name: Xilem
description: Raph Levien's successor to Druid, architectural showcase, still early

first-release.comment: First crates.io publish (0.1.0) on May 7, 2024. Raph Levien's original architecture blog post dates to 2022, but the crate is much younger.

maintenance-status.comment: 1,422 commits on main; most recent commits April 22, 2026. Linebender publishes monthly progress updates.

maturity.comment: README states: "Xilem can currently be considered to be in an alpha state." Still pre-1.0 (0.4.0) with expected breaking changes between minor releases.

corporate-backing.value: Linebender community. Major contributors (including creator Raph Levien and Matt Campbell of AccessKit) have received multi-year grants from Google Fonts.
corporate-backing.comment: Linebender itself is described as a volunteer community organization, not a company or foundation. Individual contributors are funded by Google Fonts.

implementation.comment: Workspace members are all Rust crates; rendering via pure-Rust Vello (wgpu).

rendering-mode.comment: Users produce a lightweight view tree that is diffed to apply minimal updates to a retained widget tree (Masonry).

reactivity-model.comment: Declarative view tree with diff-based reconciliation, described as "inspired by React, SwiftUI and Elm." Not signals-based; state lives in app struct passed through the view hierarchy.

thread-model.comment: Event loop runs on the main thread (winit); async work uses the Task/Worker view primitives which drive Tokio futures.

renderer.comment: 2D rendering via Vello (a compute-shader-based vector renderer) on top of wgpu. wgpu itself selects Vulkan/Metal/DX12/GL/WebGPU at runtime but the framework-level backend is wgpu.

gpu-acceleration.comment: Vello is a GPU-accelerated vector renderer.

hidpi-support.comment: winit reports scale factors that Masonry/Xilem propagate to rendering; v0.4 added a Length type for logical pixels.

vector-rendering.comment: Vello is explicitly a 2D vector renderer; Parley handles vector text rendering.

custom-shaders.comment: No documented public API for injecting custom wgpu render passes at the xilem layer; the canvas example uses Vello paint primitives rather than raw shaders.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: The canvas.rs example demonstrates custom 2D rendering via Vello's scene API inside the UI; raw wgpu viewport embedding is not independently verified.

windows.comment: winit supports Windows; README references Windows as a target (one debug flag is noted as not supported on Windows, which implies Windows is otherwise a target).

macos.comment: README gives macOS Cargo config recommendations; winit supports macOS.

linux-x11.comment: README enumerates Linux/BSD package requirements; winit supports X11 by default.

linux-wayland.comment: README mentions both Wayland and X11 Linux/BSD support; winit provides native Wayland.

ios.comment: No iOS example or documented support; Android is the sole official mobile target in the 2024/2025 roadmap.

android.comment: Dedicated xilem/examples/android directory with cargo-apk glue code for calc, emoji_picker, http_cats, mason, stopwatch, to_do_mvc, variable_clock. Android is a funded focus per the Linebender roadmap; April 2025 update added software-keyboard text input and a11y on Android.

web-wasm.comment: Separate xilem_web crate in the workspace with its own examples (counter, fetch, todomvc, svg demos). README: "It has a web backend and a Masonry backend."

embedded-no-std.comment: Depends on tokio, winit, vello, and wgpu — none of which are no_std compatible.

authoring-style.comment: Views are constructed via Rust functions/builder methods (flex((button(...), label(...))) style); no DSL or macro syntax for markup.

hot-reload.comment: No hot-reload feature documented or in release notes; Rust recompile required.

layout-system.comment: Flex (row/column with MainAxis/CrossAxis alignment), Grid, ZStack, Split, SizedBox primitives. The Flex model is the dominant idiom; Grid is also provided. No constraints solver.

styling-approach.comment: Masonry's properties module defines theme defaults and per-widget property sets; v0.4 added styling properties to Xilem views. No CSS-like stylesheet DSL.

theme-switching.comment: Masonry exposes theme defaults but runtime light/dark switching is not independently verified in docs or examples.

accessibility.comment: README: "AccessKit for plugging into accessibility APIs." Masonry Core builds accessibility trees via AccessKit.

internationalization.comment: Parley provides complex-script text shaping (RTL, BiDi), but Xilem ships no built-in localization/message-catalog framework.

widget-breadth.comment: Covers basics (Button, Label, Checkbox, Slider, TextInput, ProgressBar, Image, Spinner, Prose, Flex, Grid, SizedBox, Split, ZStack, Portal, VirtualScroll, IndexedStack, VariableLabel, Transformed) but no tables, trees, rich text/markdown, native menus, dialogs, or charts.

data-grid-table.comment: No table/grid widget; VirtualScroll provides virtualized lists for simple one-dimensional data.

rich-text-markdown.comment: Label and Prose render styled text via Parley, but there is no dedicated markdown widget or rich-text editor.

native-menus.comment: No native menu bar or context menu support.

file-dialogs.comment: No built-in file dialog widget or documented rfd integration at the xilem layer.

charts-plotting.comment: No first-party charting; custom drawing is possible via Vello's scene API (see canvas.rs example).

custom-widgets.comment: Masonry exposes a Widget trait for authoring new widgets, but Xilem is in alpha, documentation is sparse, and the trait surface is still evolving between releases.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: Tokio is a direct dependency; Task and Worker view primitives spawn Tokio futures. No support for async-std or smol is advertised.

system-tray.comment: No built-in tray icon support; would require an external crate (tray-icon).

clipboard.comment: v0.4.0 release notes: "Basic text-only clipboard operations." No image or rich-content clipboard yet.

drag-and-drop.comment: No documented OS-level drag-and-drop in xilem or Masonry as of v0.4.0.

multi-window.comment: v0.4.0 added initial multi-window support to Masonry; multiple_windows.rs example ships in the repo.

binary-size.comment: Not independently measured. Expected to be moderate-to-large given wgpu + Vello + Parley + winit + tokio direct deps, but no published "hello world" numbers.

startup-time.comment: Not independently measured. wgpu adapter init typically dominates cold start, similar to other wgpu-based frameworks.

idle-redraw.comment: Reactive diffing model: "lightweight view objects, diffing them to provide minimal updates to a retained UI." Redraw is triggered by state changes rather than a continuous loop.

dependency-count.comment: 7 direct deps (masonry, masonry_winit, tokio, tracing, vello, winit, xilem_core) but transitive closure via wgpu/vello/parley/winit/tokio comfortably exceeds 200 crates. Not independently measured with cargo tree.

documentation-quality.comment: API docs exist on docs.rs; README has TODO stubs (e.g., "TODO: Include note about how to use cargo apk here"). No book, limited tutorials. Reflects the explicit alpha status.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic Rust builder-style views; functional but verbose view types and explicit state threading. Core team has noted ergonomics as an active work item (view types can surface complex generics).

examples-count.comment: 23 examples in xilem/examples (calc, canvas, emoji_picker, flex, http_cats, lists, mason, memoization, multiple_windows, scroll, slider_demo, stopwatch, to_do_mvc, transforms, variable_clock, virtual_cats, widgets, etc.) plus separate xilem_web examples.

interactive-playground.comment: No official online playground or demo gallery.

ide-tooling.comment: No Xilem-specific LSP, formatter, or inspector beyond stock rust-analyzer.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: Masonry ships a widget inspector (F11) and a debug layout-rect overlay (F12); both are available to Xilem apps.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the top-level `xilem` crate as of 2026-04-24. Low because Xilem is still alpha and not yet used as a library dependency.

notable-production-users.comment: No shipping production apps widely known. A sample Mastodon client ("Placehero") ships in-tree as an early-stage showcase.

community-size.comment: Linebender Zulip is active for 2D graphics/UI topics broadly; Xilem-specific contributor base is modest (~40 contributors across a year's releases).

ecosystem-crates.comment: Part of the Linebender stack (Vello, Parley, Fontique, AccessKit, Masonry) but no widespread third-party widget or theme crates for Xilem itself.
